source: Generated from a per-system Morphous manifest and prompt records. CSS variables are shadcn/tweakcn-compatible.
typography: Slender English workflow UI with stable tabular numbers, narrow task labels, and Noto Sans JP guidance for Japanese queue, approval, handoff, and status screens.
layout: Airy 8px workflow grids, vertical stem-line dividers, mycelium-thread separators, compact queue cards, dashboard/data panels, command search, task tables, and restrained 8px cards.

prompts.label: Transparent enoki cluster motif
prompts.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous transparent motif chroma-key source
Primary request: Create only an enoki mushroom cluster for a Morphous design-system catalog, isolated as a single complete fungi subject on a removable chroma-key background.
Subject: A dense winter-wood enoki cluster with many very slender ivory stems, small pale gold to honey caps, subtle cream gills visible on a few caps, delicate bundled vertical growth, slight translucent stem shine, and a compact natural base with warm wood-brown root shading. The identity must read clearly as enoki mushrooms, not generic button mushrooms, oyster mushrooms, morels, coral fungus, noodles, flowers, or wheat.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; include the full cluster silhouette from base to cap tips without cropping. Clean studio lighting that preserves ivory stems, pale gold caps, gill detail, and slender workflow-like vertical rhythm. No cast shadow.
Transparent workflow: place the subject on a perfectly flat solid #00ff00 chroma-key background for removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, lighting variation, or texture. Do not use #00ff00 anywhere in the subject.
Constraints: no forest scene, no log as a prop beyond a minimal natural root base, no plate, no bowl, no hand, no knife, no label, no text, no watermark, no second species, no loose pile, no sliced mushrooms.

prompts.label: Light-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Enoki light-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive light-mode design-system board for a Morphous theme named "Morphous Enoki", derived from the enoki mushroom cluster cutout visible in this conversation. This board is a source reference for implementing a shadcn/tweakcn-compatible slender workflow system.
Canvas: 3840x2160 wide 16:9 high-resolution board, clean professional product design-system presentation. Use a precise grid, large readable labels, and avoid tiny dense text.
Required visible sections: Morphous Enoki, Enoki Cluster, Palette, Typography, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Workflow Dashboard / Data, Forms, Tables, Command / Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadows.
Reference use: Extract palette, texture rhythm, contrast, spacing mood, and interaction language from ivory enoki stems, pale gold caps, cream gills, honey cap highlights, warm winter-wood root base, subtle mycelium fibers, vertical bundled growth, and slender workflow rhythm. Use only motif-derived colors plus near-neutrals taken from the motif: spore white, stem ivory, mycelium cream, pale cap gold, honey cap ochre, gill taupe, winter bark brown, root umber, wood ink. Do not introduce unrelated colors.
Typography: English product-workflow UI examples with Japanese typography guidance. Include short readable labels such as Geist, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, Noto Sans JP, 日本語 UI, 見出し, 本文, ラベル, 数値. Use compact bilingual labels for workflow queues, approvals, tasks, handoffs, and operating metrics.
UI coverage: primary/secondary/ghost/destructive buttons, hover/pressed/disabled/loading states, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, queue cards, kanban lane chips, workflow timeline, sidebar navigation, top navigation, breadcrumb, dashboard metric cards, line chart, bar chart, donut chart, task table rows, command search palette, filters, empty/loading/error states, progress, stepper, date picker, status chips, spacing scale, 8px radius samples, border, shadow, subtle texture samples, icon buttons, theme token swatches, CSS variable snippets.
Style: slender workflow system, precise 8px grid and 8px card radius, airy ivory surfaces, pale gold primary actions, winter-bark text and borders, honey cap active states, mycelium-thread dividers, fine vertical stem-line texture, compact but scannable dashboards, polished shadcn/tweakcn component language.
Constraints: no generic purple or blue gradients, no neon colors, no beige-only palette, no mushroom forest scene, no food packaging, no restaurant menu, no random brand names, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Enoki", no tiny unreadable paragraphs.

prompts.label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Enoki dark-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive dark-mode design-system board for the Morphous theme named "Morphous Enoki" / "Enoki Cluster". Translate the same light design-system language visible in this conversation into dark mode; do not invent a new unrelated theme.
Canvas: 3840x2160 wide 16:9 high-resolution board, clean professional shadcn/tweakcn design-system presentation, slender workflow system mood. Keep labels large and legible.
Required visible sections: Morphous Enoki, Enoki Cluster, Dark Mode, Palette, Typography, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Workflow Dashboard / Data, Forms, Tables, Command / Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadows.
Reference use: Preserve the enoki motif-derived palette roles from ivory stems, pale gold caps, cream gills, honey cap highlights, warm winter-wood root base, subtle mycelium fibers, vertical bundled growth, and slender workflow rhythm. Use only motif-derived colors plus near-neutrals taken from the motif: deep winter wood, root umber, bark brown, gill taupe, stem ivory, mycelium cream, pale cap gold, honey ochre, spore white. The board must read as enoki cluster, not porcini, morel, oyster mushroom, restaurant, forest, or generic black-and-gold UI.
Typography: English product-workflow UI examples with Japanese typography guidance. Include short readable labels such as Geist, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, Noto Sans JP, 日本語 UI, 見出し, 本文, ラベル, 数値. Use compact bilingual labels for workflow queues, approvals, tasks, handoffs, and operating metrics.
UI coverage: primary/secondary/tertiary/ghost/destructive buttons, hover/pressed/disabled/loading states, checkbox, radio, switch, input, select, textarea, tabs, badges, alerts, queue cards, kanban lane chips, workflow timeline, sidebar navigation, top navigation, breadcrumb, dashboard metric cards, line chart, bar chart, donut chart, task table rows, command search palette, filters, empty/loading/error states, progress, stepper, date picker, status chips, texture samples, radius samples, spacing scale, shadow samples, token code snippets, icon buttons, theme token swatches.
Visual details: deep winter-wood background, root-umber panels, ivory foreground text, pale-gold primary actions, honey cap chart highlights, gill-taupe borders, mycelium-thread separators, subtle vertical stem-line texture overlays, thin cap-gold focus rings, restrained contrast, crisp 8px shadcn/tweakcn controls and dashboards.
Constraints: no neon orange, no generic pure-black gaming UI, no purple or blue gradient dominance, no unrelated saturated colors, no mushroom forest scene, no restaurant menu, no food packaging, no random brand names, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Enoki", no tiny unreadable paragraphs.
